observations.recommendation: a. Protect and secure ownership over all properties and assets for which the Municipality has a legal and valid claim by having it registered under the Torrens System; and
b. Direct the Disposal Committee to periodically reassess the status and condition of the properties in the municipality particularly on its serviceability; and
c. Direct the Disposal Committee to prepare the WMR and the IIRUP and follow the disposal procedures in accordance COA-DBM-Joint Circular No. 2024-01.
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to firmly impose and collect liquidated damages against contractors for the delays in the implementation and completion of projects;
observations.reason: No liquidated damages were imposed/collected in final billing of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: b. Impose administrative sanctions against the contractor and to parties responsible for its non-completion;
c. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to consider the possible blacklisting of the erring contractors.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance collection efforts so that RPT and SET Receivables are realized, tax delinquencies are collected, and impose all administrative action to enforce payment on delinquent RPT thru levy on real property or by judicial action as provided in Section 256 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Delinquent real property taxes were not yet collected in full and no judicial actions were initiated by the management.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; and
observations.reason: One lot is registered in the name of the Municipal Government of Dao, Capiz title no. 089-2022000592. For other lots there were still documents that are lacking especially in LRA Manila, Quezon City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a.1 Compute for the annual depreciation expense for all Property,
Plant, and Equipment (PPE) items in accordance with Paragraph
3 of IPSAS 17, which requires depreciation to commence when the
asset is available for use;
a.2 Record the depreciation expense of Road Network to Depreciation
Expense-Infrastructure Assets accounts;
a.3 Make adjusting entry to correct the accumulated depreciation of
the affected asset accounts due to erroneous provision and
misclassification of depreciation expense since CY 2016 with a net
amount of ₱22.856 million; and
b. Direct the General Services Officer to immediately submit the necessary
supporting documents for all donated properties, including Deeds of
Donation, Inventory Reports, and Acknowledgment Receipts, to the
Accounting Office for proper valuation and recording.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the General Services Officer to prepare and issue the
corresponding Inventory Custodian Slips for the 27 units tables to
establish accountability over the properties;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply the prescribed
account titles and codes for Semi-Expendable Properties set forth in the
COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; and
c. Instruct every requesting officer or end users to itemize and indicate the
specification of the needed items in the Purchase Request as basis for
the preparation of Purchase Order.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Accountant
to:
a. Revisit the description of accounts set forth in the Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2025;
b. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to correct the overstatement of Repairs
and Maintenance accounts amounting to ₱306,387.50 and to reclassify
the same amount to the appropriate asset and expense accounts to
ensure accurate classification and fair presentation of accounts in the
financial statements; and
We further recommended and the Management agreed to direct the GSO
to:
a. Prepare and issue the Inventory Custodian Slip for semi-expendable
properties identified in Table 1 to establish proper accountability over
the properties.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Engineer to immediately submit Inspection Report of
infrastructure projects to the Accounting Office upon expiration of
contracts and during year-end; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to record the assets and the corresponding
liabilities upon receipt of the Inspection Report.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Make the necessary adjustments to the LGU books to correct the
erroneous/incomplete entries during CY 2024;
b. Exert extra efforts in identifying the remaining discrepancies between the
two accounts in all funds.

We recommended, and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the:
a. MGSO to:
a.1 prepare the RPCI and submit the same to the accounting office and
the audit team; and
a.2 moving forward, ensure the timely submission of the monthly SSMI
and the semi-annual RPCI.
b. Municipal Accountant to:
b.1 effect adjustments to the inventory accounts based on the RPCI
prepared by the MGSO; and
b.2 periodically conduct reconciliation of MGSO records with the
accounting records to maintain an accurate valuation of the
Inventory account balances.

We recommended, and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the:
a. LDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plans for
unexpended LDRRMF balances based on the year of transfer, for ease in
monitoring the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF balances; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
b.1 To work closely with the LDRRM Officer in identifying and recording
the LDRRM related transactions; and
b.2 Maintain a Subsidiary Ledgers for the unutilized DRRMF based on the
year of transfer.

We recommended, and the GAD Focal Person agreed to:
a. Include the HGDG training in the CY 2025 GAD Plan and Budget (GPB)
as mandated by item 5(b) of Section 4.1.C.4 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01, if necessary; and
b. Use the HGDG test in determining the attribution rate of the GAD fund
in the LGU’s various Programs, Projects, and Activities on the succeeding
GPBs and GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended, and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the:
a. Municipal General Services Office to identify the lots wherein municipal
roads are constructed;
b. Municipal Assessor to determine the values of the land identified as Road
Lot components; and
c. Municipal Accountant to record the Road Lot component of municipal
roads as valued by the Municipal Assessor;

We also recommended and management committed that the LGU expedite the
acquisition of the identified Road Lots.
b. Require the Property Unit to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Inventory Committee and Accounting for the necessary adjustments in the land area and amount, if any.
a. Create a Disposal Committee following the provisions contained in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties to facilitate the disposal of the identified unserviceable properties;
a. Strictly comply with the submission of the documentary requirements as provided in COA Circular 2012-01 to establish the propriety and validity of all disbursements; and
b. Monitor the repairs and maintenance of equipment and vehicle by keeping a subsidiary ledger and the like to track the repair history of each item.

a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to identify the specific projects to be included in the 20% DF and recommend only the specific program, project and activity that partakes the nature of an investments and capital expenditures pursuant to Article 454 (d) of Rules and Regulations Implementing the LGC;
b. Require LDC and the Municipal Accountant to justify why contracts for the aforementioned PPAs were executed and disbursed without prior approval from the SB;

c. Obtain approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to the implementation of specific programs/projects/activities funded out of lump sum appropriation under the 20% DF pursuant to Article 4 (d) of the Rules and Regulation Implementing the Local Government Code (LGC) and Item 3.1.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 as clarified in COA Memorandum 2010-014 dated April 22, 2010; and
d. Ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.
a. Require the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their PPE records and effect the necessary adjustments, if any, in their respective records;
b. Direct the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update PC and PPELC, per category of PPE, respectively, pursuant to Section 119 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Oblige the Property Custodian to update all unserviceable AREs to properly account and identify the properties and the persons responsible therefor;

d. Require the Property Custodian to account for all unserviceable properties and facilitate the preparation of IIRUP in order for the Disposal Committee to expedite disposal of the unserviceable properties and equipment before losing its value due to rapid deterioration.
a. Require the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their PPE records and effect the necessary adjustments, if any, in their respective records;
b. Direct the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update PC and PPELC, per category of PPE, respectively, pursuant to Section 119 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Oblige the Property Custodian to update all AREs to properly account and identify the properties and the persons responsible therefor;

e. Require the Property Custodian to account for all unserviceable properties and facilitate the preparation of IIRUP in order for the Disposal Committee to expedite disposal of the unserviceable properties and equipment before losing its value due to rapid deterioration.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to firmly impose and collect liquidated damages against contractors for the delays in the implementation and completion of projects;
observations.reason: No liquidated damages were imposed/collected in final billing of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: d. Impose administrative sanctions against the contractor and to parties responsible for its non-completion; and
e. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to consider the possible blacklisting of the erring contractors.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance collection efforts so that RPT and SET Receivables are realized, tax delinquencies are collected, and impose all administrative action to enforce payment on delinquent RPT thru levy on real property or by judicial action as provided in Section 256 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Delinquent real property taxes were not yet collected in full and no judicial actions were initiated by the management.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; and
observations.reason: Waiting for one title to be released by the Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Record collections of receipts in period of occurrence and not from the time the collections were deposited; b. To disclose the breakdown of the CCE in the Notes to Financial Statements and other accounts that purport to be disclosed; c. To establish task force or team to conduct the BRS of the prior years and submit copy of the task force to Audit Team; and d. To regularly conduct the monthly BRS of the depository bank accounts with the cash book balance.
observations.reason: No permanent employees qualified for the task. No regular bank recon is being conducted

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Protect and secure ownership over all properties and assets for which the Municipality has a legal and valid claim by having it registered under the Torrens System; and
b. Require the Property Unit to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Inventory Committee and Accounting for the necessary adjustments in the land area and amount, if any.

2.7 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Create a Disposal Committee following the provisions contained in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties to facilitate the disposal of the identified unserviceable properties;
b. Direct the Disposal Committee to periodically reassess the status and condition of the properties in the municipality particularly on its serviceability; and
c. Direct the Disposal Committee to prepare the WMR and the IIRUP and follow the disposal procedures in accordance COA-DBM-Joint Circular No. 2024-01

3.6 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Strictly comply with the submission of the documentary requirements as provided in COA Circular 2012-01 to establish the propriety and validity of all disbursements; and
b. Monitor the repairs and maintenance of equipment and vehicle by keeping a subsidiary ledger and the like to track the repair history of each item.

1.17 We recommended, and the management agreed, that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Facilitate completion of the requirements, such as the reconciliation
of recorded balances of PPEs in the FS and the RPCPPE, among
others, to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts provided
under COA Circular No. 2020-006;

b. Make the necessary adjustments on the erroneous recording of
depreciation for various PPEs;

c. Assign monetary values on the parcels of land donated to the
Municipality.

2.14 We recommended, and the management agreed, that the LCE direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Exert effort in locating the Fund Utilization Reports for the various
fund transfers from National Government Agencies and record the
same in the Municipality’s books;

b. Facilitate the refund of unused balances to the Source Agencies;

c. Review the basis for the negative balances and make the necessary
corrections, if warranted; and

d. Make the necessary adjustments to the Due to NGAs and the Trust
Liability – DRRMF accounts.

3.12 We recommended, and the management agreed, that the LCE direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Strictly require the liquidation of all cash advances as of year-end;

b. Reconcile the prior years’ unliquidated balance of ₱90,443.56; and

c. Make the necessary adjustments for the ₱12,300.00 reconciling item.

4.9 We recommended, and the management agreed, that the LCE direct:

a. The Municipal Engineer to submit additional approved
Variation/Suspension Orders, if any for the projects that incurred
delays in completion, otherwise, Liquidated Damages should be
demanded and collected from the contractor; and

b. The Municipal Accountant to demand the submission of the
Statement of Time Elapsed upon processing of progress/final
payments.

4.10 Moving Forward, we recommended, and management agreed, that the
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, strictly
impose the collection of Liquidating Damages equal to 1/10 of 1 percent of the
cost of unfinished portion for each day of delay upon contractors.

5.9 We recommended, and the management agreed, that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to immediately act on the unimplemented and
delayed projects;

b. Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement and
implementation process to realize the programmed projects as
planned; and

c. Municipal Budget Officer to include the identified PPAs in the
subsequent Annual Procurement Plan to facilitate its
implementation;

5.10 Moving forward, we recommended, and the management agreed, that the
management optimally utilize the 20% DF and ensure that development
projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready.

6.7 We recommended ,and the management agreed, that the LCE direct both the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:

a. reconcile the prior years’ net outstanding balance of ₱210,788.13;

b. remit immediately the reconciled balance of GSIS, PhilHealth and
Due to Pag-IBIG accounts as of December 31, 2023, if any.

7.9 We recommended, and the management agreed, the LCE direct both the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:

a. conduct a reconciliation of the prior years’ outstanding balance of
₱135,238.98; and

b. remit immediately the unremitted balance for CY 2023 of
₱859,750.31 along with the reconciled balance of Due to BIR account
as of December 31, 2023

7.10 Moving forward, to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD No.
1445 and BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98, by remitting on time fund due to
BIR to avoid the incurrence of interest, penalties and/or surcharges.

8.7 We recommended, and the management agreed, that the LCE direct:

a. The MSWDO to prepare the necessary documents on the receipt and
issuance of donations from DSWD as required by COA Circular No.
2014-002, and its timely submission to the MGSO for recording;

b. The MGSO to prepare the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for
DRRM; and

c. The Municipal Accountant to disclose receipts and distributions of
donated relief goods in the Notes to Financial Statements based on
the registry submitted by the MGSO.
d. Direct the Inventory Committee to improve procedures in the conduct of physical count following the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2022-006; and

a. Direct the Head of Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to closely supervise and certify project accomplishments based on validated completed work items and enforce the timely and full completion of the project within the target contract duration;

b. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to closely monitor the project implementation in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract, MOA or other relevant issuances for the projects;
We recommended and Management agreed to include only items and expenditures that are chargeable to LDRRMF and those included in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: Henceforth, Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer should strictly include only activities that adhere to guidelines in the preparation and utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to effect the withholding of creditable VAT and other percentage taxes on payments of goods and services and to hold the persons responsible liable for its omission.

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to justify erroneous charging and entries to the PPA’s appropriated and classifications of expenses related to Generation, Transmission and Distribution and affected Extraordinary and Miscellaneous expenses;

b. Stop the practice of charging expenses not in accordance with the description of accounts and the incurrence of obligations be spent solely for the purpose intended. Henceforth, strictly adhere the account description as provided in COA Circular No. 2015-014.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to reconcile the variances between the actual counts and recorded PPEs; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to review and draw adjusting entries for the discrepancies in the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) requires the Municipal Accountant to:

a. exert more effort to identify the remaining unaccounted cash advances totaling ₱826,577.05, and immediately apply appropriate actions to settle the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer be directed to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System and to execute their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.reason: Components of the project are not properly or cannot be identified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require:

a. the concerned personnel to expedite the processing of land title in the name of the municipality; and

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts in the General Fund, Trust Fund, and Special Education Fund books and effect adjustments to correct the account balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be substantially complied, thus, require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

Likewise, enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on Local Road Network and provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the original copies of cancelled checks.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking DVs together with the supporting documents.
Management submits the lacking documents pursuant to Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012- 001 dated June 14, 2012.
The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant:

To coordinate with the MBO in reconciling the Accounts Payable and its listing and effect the corresponding adjustment, if any;

To review the long outstanding payables and make necessary adjustment for reversal if deemed; and

To maintain the respective Subsidiary Ledgers for Accounts Payable, Due to Officers and Employees and Other GL accounts which is deemed necessary.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant:

a. To draw a JEV for the settlement of the trust liabilities for OCA from DOH and to adjust the erroneous entries; and

b. To review and reconcile and update the Due to NGAS account balances.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the certified copies of approved application forms for loans with existing balance as of writing;

We recommended and Management agreed to direct:

The Municipal Assessor to continuously update the assessments and classifications of the real properties and submit reports to Sangguniang Panlalawigan for the enactment and approval of general revision of property assessment;

The Municipal Treasurer to:

immediately send collection letters to the delinquent LGU Officials and provide a copy of their payments to this Office; recommend to the legislative, thru the LCE, stringent measure/remedies for the collection of taxes; reconcile with the records of the Municipal Accountant on the RPT receivables and effect the identified reconciling items.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

The MPDC to review and monitor the PPAs under 20% MDF and implement the same as planned; and

Instruct the MCT (Designate) to provide the Audit Team the updated reports on the status of the implementation of the KALAHI-CIDSS.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

a. MHO to:

1. Record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining undistributed items, moving forward; 2. Submit a copy of the same to the GSO and the Municipal Accountant within the reglementary period.

b. GSO to:

1. Assist the MHO in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, moving forward; and 2. Maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents.

3. Prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

c. Municipal Accountant to: 1. Disclose in the Notes to the FS the quantity received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the property custodian to ensure an efficient property management by
following the prescribed procedures in property recording and maintain accurate
and complete property records;
b. Assign additional personnel for the inventory committee, if needed;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the property custodian to reconcile their
balances in the books of accounts and the property records respectively and
prepare necessary adjusting entries;
d. Require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to look into and
comply with the guidelines and procedures set forth in the COA Circular No.
2020-006 to address issues and concerns on the non-existing and unserviceable
assets; and
e. Require the GSO to prepare Physical Inventory Plan and to facilitate the
completion of the one-time cleansing not to exceed the 3 months period.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with the transparency
requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 in all its infrastructure projects undertaken either by
contract or by administration. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Engineer that the required
billboard/signage be provided for all ongoing projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to prioritize the use of LDRRMF in
accordance with laws, rules, and regulations to support disaster mitigation,
preparation, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to justify erroneous
charging and entries to the PPA’s appropriated and classifications of expenses
related to Generation, Transmission and Distribution and Extraordinary and
Miscellaneous expenses; and
b. Stop the practice of charging expenses not in accordance with the description of
accounts and the incurrence of obligations be spent solely for the purpose
intended. Henceforth, strictly adhere the account description as provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-014.
a. Direct the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386;
observations.reason: Still on process, requires submission with additional documents

observations.recommendation: b. Require the Municipal Accountant to book-up the market/appraised value of lots acquired in the books of the Municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

a. Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs in accordance with the socio-economic development, environmental management and disaster response requirements provided by DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG and DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020;

b. Include specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality;
c. Come up with strategies/measures to fast tract/facilitate completion of identified 20% DF projects; and
d. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to comply with her duties and functions as stipulated in Section 472 of the Local Government Code and Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations;
b. Provide manpower to hire services of a qualified and able tax mapper to conduct the tax mapping activity, ocular inspection and other relevant functions of the said office;
c. Submit updated Report on Assessment to the Provincial Assessor’s Office to provide them an accurate information relevant to their plan for a possible general revision; and
d. Furnish the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the updated and accurate data on RPT Assessment which serves as basis for RPT target and collection every year.

a. Improve its collection effort by regularly and properly sending out notices to delinquent taxpayers; and b. Enforce collection remedies provided by RA No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction. In order to demonstrate the Municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Direct the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by Municipality
under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to book-up the market/appraised value of
lots acquired in the booksof the Municipality in order to fairly present the
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts
in the financial statements.
AOM No. 2021-001 dated February 9, 2021was issued for this audit observation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

a. Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation,
otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs in
accordance with the socio-economic development, environmental
management and disaster response requirements provided by DILG and DBM
JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG and DBM JMC No. 01 dated
March 27, 2020;
b. Include specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for the
20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to
maximize the allocated funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-
economic development of the Municipality;
c. Come up with strategies/measures to fast tract/facilitate completion of
identified 20% DF projects; and
d. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in
formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its
constituents.
AOM No. 2021-002 dated February 9, 2021 was issued for this audit observation.

We recommended and theManagement agreed to the following:

a. Require the Municipal Assessor to comply with her duties and functions as
stipulated in Section 472 of the Local Government Code and Manual on Real
Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations;
b. Provide manpower to hire services of a qualified and able tax mapperto
conduct the tax mapping activity, ocular inspection and other relevant
functions of the said office;
c. Submit updated Report on Assessment to the Provincial Assessor’s Office to
provide them an accurate information relevant to their plan for a possible
general revision; and
d. Furnish the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the updated and accurate data
on RPTAssessmentwhich serves as basis for RPT target and collection every
year.
AOM No. 2021-003 dated February 9, 2021 was issued for this audit observation.

a. Require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; b. Require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens Title for the LGU’s real estate; and c. Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary entries to book-up the market/appraised value in the books of the municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Unavailability of records/data; lots for survey and approval; Scheduled the activities for survey of lots needed to secure certification from DPWH in March to April 2020 however, because of COVID-19 pandemic, it was delayed.
The titling of the parcel of land in the name of the Municipality is in process.
Reiterated in this year’s audit report.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records/data; lots for survey and approval; Scheduled the activities for survey of lots needed to secure certification from DPWH in March to April 2020 however, because of COVID-19 pandemic, it was delayed.

observations.recommendation: a. Include specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality; b. Adjust strategies/measures that will fast tract/facilitate completion of identified 20% DF projects; and c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the required documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate the review and verification of the same and the timely preparation of the required reports.

a. Management advise the Administrative Officers of every department to be more careful in approving the Loan Applications of employees under their jurisdiction, making sure that after amortizations of the loan applied for are deducted, the same will not bring the Net Take Home Pay of the employee to below the minimum required by the law; and b. The Municipal Accountant, in signing the Certificate of Net Pay of employees and upon determining that the Net Take Home Pay will fall below what is required by law, should include a phrase stating that the same is not applicable for loan purposes, or any other similarly applicable notes, in order to strictly implement related provisions and restrictions of the law.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by municipality
under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens
Title for the LGU’s real estate; and
c. Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, the Municipal Accountant should prepare
the necessary entries to book-up the market/appraised value in the books of the
municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)-
Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

AOM No. 2020-003 dated January 22, 2020 was issued for this audit observation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Include specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for the 20% DF
which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated
funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the
Municipality;
b. Adjust strategies/measures that will fast tract/facilitate completion of identified 20%
DF projects; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in
formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

AOM No. 2020-004 dated January 22, 2020 was issued for this audit observation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Improve its collection effort by regularly and properly sending out notices to
delinquent taxpayers; and
b. Enforce collection remedies provided by RA No. 7160, particularly the sale of
delinquent properties through public auction. In order to demonstrate the
municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property
taxation and deter tax evasions.

AOM No. 2020-005 dated January 23, 2020 was issued for this audit observation.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to submit the required documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period
to facilitate the review and verification of the same and the timely preparation of the
required reports.

AOM No. 2020-002 dated January 22, 2020 was issued for this audit observation.

We recommended that:
a. Management advise the Administrative Officers of every department to be more
careful in approving the Loan Applications of employees under their jurisdiction,
making sure that after amortizations of the loan applied for are deducted, the same
will not bring the Net Take Home Pay of the employee to below the minimum
required by the law; and
b. The Municipal Accountant, in signing the Certificate of Net Pay of employees and
upon determining that the Net Take Home Pay will fall below what is required by
law, should include a phrase stating that the same is not applicable for loan
purposes, or any other similarly applicable notes, in order to strictly implement
related provisions and restrictions of the law.

AOM No. 2020-001 dated January 22, 2020 was issued for this audit observation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Effect full and timely implementation of the projects embodied in the AIP; and
Make immediate realignment, if planned programs and projects are no longer feasible or could not be implemented, so that the amount will be utilized for other relevant projects.
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant book up the purchased lot located at Barangay Agtambi with an area of 20,000 sq.m in the land account of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386;
Require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens Title for the LGU’s real estate;  and
Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, the Municipal Accountant should  prepare the necessary entries to book-up the market/appraised value in the books of the municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

Require the focal person that during identification of PPA’s for budgeting, propose only those which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership.

Strictly implement all the projects, programs and activities in relation to its Annual Budget; and

See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality in order to fully establish ownership thereof for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Require the focal person that during identification of PPA’s for budgeting, propose only those which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership.
Strictly implement all the projects, programs and activities in relation to its Annual Budget; and
See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality in order to fully establish ownership thereof for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386;
Require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure Torrens Title for the LGU’s real estate;  and
Upon the completion of the Torrens Title, the Municipal Accountant should  prepare the necessary entries to book-up the market/appraised value in the books of the municipality in order to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)- Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

Strictly implement all the PPAs in the approved GAD Plan and Budget; and

See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed.
observations.managementAction: Six out of ten Program, Projects and Activities (PPAs) not implemented in 2016 were carried as approved PPAs in 2017.
observations.reason: However, there are still PPAs that the management failed to implement.

observations.recommendation:
Submit to the Audit Team the MOA and other related documents as required under Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review; and

Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to Leagues of Municipalities prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vive Mayor, as the case maybe.

Submit justification on the issues raised in the preceding paragraphs; and

Investigate the practice of repeatedly engaging the same set of suppliers over and over again and take appropriate actions to rule out the possibility of supplier to supplier and/or supplier to agency personnel collusion.

Management exhaust all legal means to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality in order to fully establish ownership thereof for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

Management ensure the submission of the Report on Source and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly implement all the PPAs in the approved GAD Plan and Budget; and
b. See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that
problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the MOA and other related documents as required under
Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review; and
b. Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the
aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to Leagues of
Municipalities prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor,
as the case maybe.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit justification on the issues raised in the preceding paragraphs; and
b. Investigate the practice of repeatedly engaging the same set of suppliers over and
over again and take appropriate actions to rule out the possibility of supplier to
supplier and/or supplier to agency personnel collusion.

We recommended that Management:
a. Revise/amend the previously enacted Municipal Ordinance on SWM to align/include
the penal sanctions stipulated in Section 49 of Chapter VI of RA No. 9003;
b. Once amended or revised, strictly implement the ordinance and see to it that Citation
Tickets are properly issued to violators and corresponding fines and penalties are
collected to yield increase in revenue and effectively maintain cleanliness in the
Municipality; and
c. Indicate the penal sanctions in the tarpaulin/billboard for violators and properly place
it in conspicuous places so that the public may be informed/guided accordingly.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal means to have the said
property titled in the name of the Municipality in order to fully establish ownership thereof
for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the
solid waste management program of the Municipality.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management ensure the submission of the
Report on Source and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team duly certified by the
Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to
Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

a. Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Section 4(8) and 2 of PD 1445, with the expected result of minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the effects of manpower attrition; b. Make Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) representation/arrange ment with any government bank like the LBP or the PVB for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and c. Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA to strengthen fiscal management.
observations.managementAction: a. the Municipal Mayor was given an authority to transact with Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) thru an approved resolution. b. MOA was already approved and was already submitted to LBP.

observations.recommendation: a. Amend the contract through the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to include in the terms and conditions such that the penalty for failure to pay the monthly rental in accordance with Item No. 1 of the contract. Moreover, guaranty deposits should also be considered in the preparation of the Contract to see to it that the LGU is secured from any unwarranted unpaid rentals at the expense of the government. b. Require the Market Administrator to strictly monitor the implementation of Item No. 2 of terms and condition of the contract. c. Submit the resolution of the Sangguniang Bayan suspending the 5 percent increase of market stalls. d. Direct the concerned LGU officials to require the lessee to pay the corresponding unpaid monthly rentals before any renewal of the contract; and e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers of all lessees/occupants to monitor any uncollected market rentals.
observations.managementAction: a. The contract was already amended such that all occupants were issued with a new contract effective January 2016. b. Market Administrator was obliged to monitor the monthly rentals for the implementation of Item No. of terms and condition of the contract. c. Approved resolution of the SB on the 5 percent increase of market stalls was already approved. d. Before the contract was agreed upon and approved, it was prerequisite that they have to pay the unpaid monthly balance. Hence, all occupants were made to pay the balance they were made to pay the balance. Hence, all occupants were made to pay the balance. e. The Municipal Accountant maintain complete subsidiary ledger of all lessees/occupants.

observations.recommendation: Exert extra efforts to (a) identify and reconstruct the records of the dormant receivable accounts; (b) reconcile these records against that of the concerned debtors and; (c) enforce collection by sending demand letters and/or confirmation letters. If analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off of these accounts balances with the approved resolution by the Sangguniang Bayan considering the material amount involved to the Commission on Audit pursuant to its 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures.
observations.managementAction: Non-moving dormant receivable accounts was already identified and reconciled in the books.

observations.recommendation: Exhaust all legal means to have the said property titled in the name of the Municipality in order to fully establish ownership thereof for all legal intents and purposes and to ensure the continuous implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Finding No. 3 There is a move to encourage the lot owner to sell the lot for this particular purpose.
